blocks.content: That night he opened AIM out of habit.
Daisy was online. Her away message had changed — something new. He read it twice.
It said: some things are better when you don't forget them.
He sat with that for a long time.
He knew that line. He knew where it came from — a Saturday in July, her turning onto Magnolia without looking back. And she'd put it up there for anyone to read.
He opened a new window. Typed. Deleted it. Typed something else.
I haven't forgotten any of it
He sent it before he could think too hard about it.
Three minutes passed. He watched the cursor. Then:
High school was eight months away.
He didn't know what he was going to do with that yet.
